concept:
Tested 5 ? First test to perform when a woman presents with amenorrhea: Beta-hCG; The most common cause of amenorrhea is pregnancy.
Term for heavy bleeding during and between menstrual periods: Menometrorrhagia
Cause of amenorrhea with
normal prolactin
no response to estrogen-progesterone challenge
a history of D and C is: Asherman's syndrome
Therapy for polycystic ovarian syndrome: Weight loss and OCPs
Medication used to induce ovulation: Clomiphene citrate
Diagnostic step required in a postmenopausal woman who presents with vaginal bleeding: Endometrial biopsy
Indications for medical treatment of ectopic pregnancy:
Stable
Unruptured ectopic pregnancy
Less than 3.5cm
Before 6 weeks gestation
Medical options for endometriosis:
OCPs
Danazol
GnRH agonists
A patient presents with:
Uterine bleeding at 18 weeks' gestation
No products expelled
Cervical os closed indicating: Threatened abortion
A patient presents with:
Uterine bleeding at 18 week's gestation
No products expelled
Membranes ruptured
Cervical os is open indicating: Inevitable abortion
Laproscopic findings in endometriosis usually show:
"Chocolate cysts"
powder burns
Most common location for an ectopic pregnancy is: Ampulla of the oviduct
How do you diagnose and follow a leiomyoma? Ultrasound (U/S)
You would expect a natural history of leiomyoma to: Regress after menopause
Patient has increased vaginal discharge and petechial patches in the upper vagina and cervix. This indicates: Trichomonas vaginitis
Treatment for bacterial vaginosis is: Oral or topical metronidazole
Most common cause of bloody nipple discharge is: Intraductal papilloma
Contraceptive methods that protect against PID:
OCPs
Barrier contraception
Unopposed estrogen is contraindicated in which cancers? Endometrial or estrogen receptor-positive breast cancer.
With a patient with recent PID with right upper quadrant (RUQ) pain, you should consider: Fitz-Hugh-Curtis syndrome
Breast malignancy presenting as itching, burning and erosion of the nipple indicate: Paget's disease
Annual screening for women with strong family history of ovarian cancer includes:
CA-125
Transvaginal ultrasound (U/S)
A 50 year-old woman leaks urine when laughing or coughing. What are the non surgical options?
Kegel exercizes
Estrogen
Pessaries for stress incontinence
A patient:
is 30 years old
has unpredictable urine loss
has a normal exam What are the medical options?
Anticholinergics (oxybutynin)
Beta-adrenergics (metaproterenol) for urge incontinence
Lab values suggestive of menopause are: Elevated serum FSH
Most common cause of female infertility is: Endometriosis
Two consecutive findings of atypical squamous cells of undetermined significance (ASCUS) on Pap smear. What is the followup evaluation?
Colposcopy
Endocervical curettage
Breast cancer type that increases future risk of invasive carcinoma in both breasts: Lobular carcinoma in situ
